evidence: The paper uses ranger permutation importance only for threshold-based feature selection: "genes with permutation importance value <0 were eliminated for the next iteration. The iterations ended when all the genes in the input matrix have permutation importance values ≥0." The 416 genes identified for conclusions are instead selected via "DE and DAPC analyses revealed a gene expression signature that covaried with survival (n = 416 genes, unadjusted p < .01, FDR < 0.45)." Substantive biological claims about mitotic cell cycle upregulation and disruptive selection on immune genes are grounded in GO enrichment (FDR < 0.05), PPI network enrichment (p < 1.0 × 10^-16), and classical selection analysis with standard errors and t-tests, not importance magnitude or ranking.
